content:
# H P H T

**Summary**: High Pressure High Temperature conditions.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] tested cement samples under HPHT conditions to simulate deep well environments.

- [[2015-khan-2|Khan, 2015]] evaluated cement performance under HPHT conditions (290°F and 4900 psi).

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] Standard conditions for deep, challenging oil and gas wells.

- [[2015-khan-2|Khan, 2015]] evaluated cement performance under HPHT conditions (290°F and 4900 psi).

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] Simulated in a laboratory using specialized curing chambers.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Temperatures above 110°C cause C-S-H to transform into C2SH (alpha-dicalcium silicate hydrate). ([[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015, p. 112]])


- Class G cement showed significant strength degradation when exposed to temperatures exceeding 230°F. ([[2015-khan-2|Khan, 2015, p. 99]])
